creator: hsu@clinet.fi
text:
Running pine compiled for 1.1.5.1 it goes into a loop.  Top shows it
whizzes on faster than there is cpu availble.

load averages:   1.07,  0.97,  0.67                                    05:52:18
30 processes:  2 running, 27 sleeping, 1 zombie
Cpu states:  2.6% user,  4.1% nice, 91.7% system,  1.6% interrupt,  0.0% idle
Memory: 10676K Act 8852K Inact 7444K Wired 3000K Free 

  PID USERNAME PRI NICE   SIZE   RES STATE   TIME   WCPU    CPU COMMAND
  926 ctl       90    4  1392K  832K run    16:30 116.21% 116.21% pine
  941 root      42    0   484K  892K run     0:01 34.17%  6.20% top
  940 root      18    0   696K  980K sleep   0:00  3.32%  1.90% tcsh
  823 hsu       18    0   692K  768K sleep   0:00  0.00%  0.00% tcsh
  804 root      18    0   704K  624K sleep   0:01  0.00%  0.00% tcsh
  868 ctl       18    0   808K  500K sleep   0:01  0.00%  0.00% bash
  787 hsu       18    0   688K  264K sleep   0:01  0.00%  0.00% <tcsh>
   56 root      18    0   376K  132K sleep   0:03  0.00%  0.00% cron

Fix: 

Don't know about pine (we probably recompile).  But the
	top figures seem to be something what needs work?
How-To-Repeat: 
	Take 1.1.5.1 binary and run it on current (our copy is a bit
old, three weeks I think, though).
